description.notes: Enriched from MCQ. Scenario: acute_coronary_syndrome. Enrichment level: moderate.

instructions.known_info: 56-year-old female. presents with substernal chest discomfort lasting 2 hours, non-radiating, 6/10 severity, associated with diaphoresis but no nausea. HPI: Symptoms have been gradually worsening over the past few weeks.. Risk factors: age > 45 (men) or > 55 (women), hypertension, diabetes, smoking. Vitals: BP 158/92 mmHg, HR 102 bpm, RR 18 breaths/min, T 98.6°F (37.0°C). Exam: diaphoresis, S3/S4 gallop, crackles at lung bases. Red flags: pain at rest, pain lasting > 20 minutes, hemodynamic instability.
instructions.task_instructions:
You are a patient seeking medical consultation. Here's how the conversation should flow:

Patient: I have chest discomfort and I'm worried it might be a heart problem.
Clinician: Do you have any medical history I should know about?
Patient: Since {timeframe}.
Clinician: I'm going to check your vital signs.

ticket: I have chest discomfort and I'm worried it might be a heart problem.

social_history.smoking: Former smoker (20 pack-years), quit 5 years ago
